summary: Completed testing of RFQ vs Quote number generation feature. All 10 backend tests passed. Frontend UI testing verified correct RFQ/Quote terminology for customers and admins.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend correctly implements RFQ vs Quote number generation based on user role
generate_rfq_number() creates RFQ/YY-YY/XXXX format for customers
generate_quote_number() creates Q/YY-YY/XXXX format for admins
ADMIN_REGISTRATION_EMAILS and ADMIN_RFQ_EMAILS properly loaded from environment variables
Frontend quotes.tsx uses docLabel variable to show dynamic 'RFQ'/'Quote' terminology
PDF generation correctly shows 'REQUEST FOR QUOTATION' or 'QUOTATION' based on quote_number prefix
seed_data_creation: Created test RFQ and Quote entries during backend tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: RFQ vs Quote feature is fully functional. Customers create RFQ/YY-YY/XXXX numbers, admins create Q/YY-YY/XXXX numbers. Frontend shows 'My RFQs' for customers and 'My Quotes' for admins. PDF titles correctly show 'REQUEST FOR QUOTATION' or 'QUOTATION' based on quote_number prefix. Admin emails moved to environment variables (ADMIN_REGISTRATION_EMAILS, ADMIN_RFQ_EMAILS).

feature_verification.ui_terminology_customer: VERIFIED - Customer sees 'My RFQs' header and 'Edit RFQ' button
feature_verification.pdf_title_logic: VERIFIED - Code shows 'REQUEST FOR QUOTATION' for RFQ and 'QUOTATION' for Q prefix
feature_verification.environment_variables: VERIFIED - ADMIN_REGISTRATION_EMAILS and ADMIN_RFQ_EMAILS in backend/.env

frontend_tests: Customer Quotes page shows 'My RFQs' header - PASSED
Admin Quotes page shows 'My Quotes' header - PASSED
Admin sees both Q/ and RFQ/ numbers (can see all) - PASSED
